steps.title: Set up the slope-intercept form with known values
steps.content: We have slope $m = -\frac{1}{2}$ and the line passes through $(6, 4)$. Use the slope-intercept form $y = mx + b$ and substitute the point to find the y-intercept.

steps.title: Solve for the y-intercept and write the equation
steps.content:
Substitute $(6, 4)$ into $y = mx + b$:
$$4 = -\frac{1}{2}(6) + b$$
$$4 = -3 + b$$
$$b = 7$$
The equation is $y = -\frac{1}{2}x + 7$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
